content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=One-line roles of v3 memory data model components summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, section `8. Модель данных в одной строке` gives a compact role map: `Source` is what was said or seen; `Entity` is who or what it is; `Note` is important and useful context not yet cemented as fact; `Claim` is a normalized fact; `Profile` is a compact projection of stable context; `Task` is what must be done; `Episode` is what the system learned; and `Me...[truncated 35 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`. entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1>,entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<7> keywords=Source,Entity,Note,Claim,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun,one-line model,memory data model tags=doc_digest,data_model,component_roles evidence=- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено - `Entity` = кто/что это вообще - `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт - `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note schema and relationship tables summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, the `Note` schema combines identity, type, title/summary, scope, status, retrieval and maturity states, anchor entity, keywords/tags, candidate claim hints, metadata, confidence/importance, validity windows, supersession, run lineage, embeddings, usage tracking, validation, evidence counts, and archival timestamps. Related tables `note_entities`, `note_s...[truncated 127 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, especially recommended fields and additional ta...[truncated 5 chars] keywords=Note schema,note_entities,note_sources,note_links,candidate_claims_json,retrieval_state,maturity_state,evidence_count tags=data_model,note_schema,relationships useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory data model layered architecture summary=In `v3`, the memory data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle / retention, and repairability by splitting responsibilities into layers: evidence layer (`Source`), graph-shaped semantic layer (`Entity`, `Claim`), rich intermediate layer (`Note`), projections / operational memory (`Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`), a...[truncated 29 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, especially the general idea for the...[truncated 22 chars] keywords=v3,memory data model,typed memory,lineage,temporal truth,Source,Entity,Claim,Note,Profile,Task,Episode tags=data_model,architecture,memory_v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Entity as canonical memory node with aliases and reversible merges summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims, tasks, notes, and episodes. It supports aliases and merge workflows, with invariants that merges do not delete history, aliases point to the current canonical entity, and erroneous merges must be reversible through an audit trail. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`. keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,aliases,merge,audit trail,claims,tasks,notes,episodes tags=doc_digest,data_model,entity,canonical_node useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note as intermediate semantic memory fragment summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment: not a `Source`, not a final `Claim`, and not chain-of-thought. It is meant for important, retrieval-useful context that should not yet be cemented as a hard fact, such as design directions, decision rationales, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, especially the definition and intended use case...[truncated 11 chars] keywords=Note,semantic memory fragment,Source,Claim,design direction,decision rationale,hypotheses,doc digests tags=data_model,note,semantic_memory useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note invariants emphasize self-contained semantic fragments summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` invariants require one note to represent one semantic fragment, remain understandable without the original chat, add value beyond duplicating a source, treat `candidate_claims_json` only as hints, and allow a useful retrieval note to persist long-term without becoming a claim. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, specifically the Note invariants. keywords=Note invariants,semantic fragment,self-contained,candidate_claims_json,retrieval,claim tags=data_model,note_invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim and Note have distinct memory roles summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Claim` is for precise factual answers, predicate-rule reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status answers, while `Note` is for richer retrieval, decision history, rationale, partial or evolving meaning, and later consolidation. The section warns that storing everything as `Claim` creates false precision, while storing everything as `Note` mak...[truncated 19 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, covering the role split between Claim and N...[truncated 4 chars] keywords=Claim,Note,false precision,soft memory,reconciliation,profile updates,rationale tags=doc_digest,memory_model,claim_vs_note useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.8,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>"},{"link_type":"supports","link_weight":0.7,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<3>"},{"link_type":"related","link_weight":0.6,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<5>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Candidate captures a distinct compact one-line glossary for all core data model components; existing notes cover architecture or specific entities but not this role map.","target_note_id":null}]}
